skill.name: godot-gdscript-patterns
skill.description: Provides Godot 4 GDScript architecture patterns for state machines, autoloads, resources, components, scenes, saves, and performance.
skill.summary: Apply practical Godot 4 GDScript patterns for maintainable game systems.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network access, environment access, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and a critical combination heuristic. Most alerts are false positives from markdown GDScript examples, local Godot APIs, headings, and reference links. The only actionable issue is a save-system example that teaches a hardcoded encryption passphrase.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded Encryption Passphrase in Save Example
medium_findings.description: The save-system example defines a constant passphrase named ENCRYPTION_KEY and passes it to FileAccess.open_encrypted_with_pass. This is documentation code, not executed by the skill, but it can lead users to ship a reusable secret in game code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded passphrase and its use in the encrypted file API are visible in the save-system example. Risk is limited because the repository contains only instructional markdown, not executable tooling.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to markdown code fences that contain GDScript examples. No shell command runner, Ruby backtick execution, or instruction to execute host commands was found in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are opening or closing markdown fences around GDScript snippets. They are documentation delimiters and do not execute commands.

low_findings.title: Network Alerts Are Local Loading or Reference Links
low_findings.description: The network findings are a Godot ResourceLoader.load_threaded_request call for local scene loading and three documentation links. No credential exfiltration, remote API call, or background network behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ResourceLoader call accepts a project resource path, and the remaining locations are markdown resource links. There is no code that sends data to a remote endpoint.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Alert Is a Variable Name
low_findings.description: The env_access finding points to a constant named ENCRYPTION_KEY in a GDScript snippet. It does not read process environment variables or platform secrets, although the hardcoded passphrase itself is tracked separately.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line declares a GDScript constant and does not use environment APIs. The naming pattern triggered the scanner, but the semantic issue is hardcoding, not environment access.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Labels Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak crypto and system reconnaissance alerts point to ordinary description text, comments, method names, and best-practice bullets. No hashing algorithm, system probing command, host inventory logic, or prompt injection attempt was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain words such as GDScript, description, duplicate_for_runtime, get_path, and decoupling. These are normal Godot documentation terms, not security-sensitive operations.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The scanner combined command, network, and credential indicators into a critical heuristic. Manual review found those indicators are unrelated false positives or non-executed examples, so malicious intent is not supported.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is instructional markdown for Godot patterns. No executable skill scripts, data exfiltration flow, credential harvesting, or prompt injection language was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Secret Pattern in Example Code
dangerous_patterns.description: A GDScript save example stores an encryption passphrase directly in source text and reuses it for save and load operations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The same constant is passed to the encrypted file API for writes and reads. The concern is instructional code quality, not active secret access by the skill.

content.value_statement: Godot projects can become hard to maintain when game systems grow without clear patterns. This skill provides practical GDScript structures for scenes, signals, state, saves, and performance.
actual_capabilities: Explains Godot 4 node, scene, resource, signal, and group concepts.
Provides state machine patterns for player and game behavior.
Shows autoload singleton and event bus patterns for global game state.
Covers resource-based data, component systems, and object pooling.
Includes scene transition, save-system, and performance guidance.
Lists best practices for typing, signals, resources, pooling, and profiling.
limitations: It is a reference skill, not a complete Godot project template.
Examples require adaptation to each project scene tree and input map.
The save encryption example should be revised before production use.
It does not validate code by running the Godot editor or tests.

use_cases.title: Prototype Player Behavior
use_cases.description: Create a clear state machine for idle, movement, attack, and jump behavior in a Godot character.

use_cases.title: Refactor Game Systems
use_cases.description: Replace tightly coupled scripts with signals, components, resources, and autoloads that are easier to maintain.

use_cases.title: Learn Godot Architecture
use_cases.description: Study practical GDScript examples for scene loading, save data, pooling, and performance habits.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how the state machine pattern in this skill works for a Godot 4 player character.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt to My Scene
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt the component and signal patterns for my Godot enemy scene with health, hitbox, and loot behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Review Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Godot game architecture and suggest where to use autoloads, resources, event buses, or object pools.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Game System
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a maintainable Godot 4 save and scene-management system using this skill, with production security concerns noted.

output_examples.input: I need a reusable architecture for player movement, attacks, and jumping.
output_examples.output: A concise state-machine plan with named states, transition rules, signals, and integration points for the player scene.

output_examples.input: My project spawns many bullets and frame rate drops during combat.
output_examples.output: An object-pooling approach that reuses inactive bullet nodes, resets runtime state, and avoids repeated allocations.

output_examples.input: I want to separate weapon stats from character scripts.
output_examples.output: A resource-based data model with editable weapon fields, runtime copies, and signal-based updates for dependent systems.

best_practices: Ask for project-specific adaptation instead of copying examples unchanged.
Use signals and resources to reduce direct scene coupling.
Review save and encryption examples before using them in production.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode real encryption secrets in exported game scripts.
Do not use autoloads for every shared value or temporary dependency.
Do not call node lookups repeatedly inside hot update loops.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a full Godot game?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and examples that you adapt to your own Godot project.

faq.question: Which Godot version does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Godot 4.x and uses GDScript syntax and APIs from that generation.

faq.question: Can it help with performance problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers pooling, cached node references, static typing, allocation reduction, and process disabling.

faq.question: Does it include save-game guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a save-system pattern, but the hardcoded passphrase example needs production review.

faq.question: Is this safe to use in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, with a marketplace warning about the hardcoded encryption passphrase in the documentation example.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The skill only links to external learning resources and does not perform network requests.
